title: Fig. 1   Fig. 2 107 Mathematics
subject: Mathematics
objective: To obtain angle bisector of an angle by paper folding
procedure:
1.   Take a thick paper and make an   ∠   ABC by paper folding (or by drawing)
and cut it out.
2.   Fold   ∠ ABC through the vertex B such that ray BA falls along ray BC.
3.   Now, unfold it.   Mark a point D anywhere on the crease as shown in
Fig. 1.
Fig. 1
4.   Make cut outs of   ∠ ABD and   ∠ DBC.
observation:
1.   Place the cut out of   ∠ ABD on   ∠ DBC or the cut out of   ∠ DBC on   ∠ ABD.
2.   ∠ ABD exactly covers   ∠ DBC.
3.   So,   ∠ ABD is equal to   ∠ DBC.
i.e., BD is the angle bisector of   ∠ ABC.
discussion:
1.   This activity can be used to measure and construct some specific
angles.
2.   Similar activity can be used to make a ‘protractor’ of 360 ° .
112 Laboratory Manual – Elementary Stage
